signals.reasoning: Cover the live SOL short. The position has now given back almost all of its peak unrealized profit, retaining only about 13%, and the board is explicitly flagging it as urgent. With no stop installed and only about 1% liquidation distance, this is protect-the-remainder position management rather than a short worth defending.

BTC is still holding the squeeze recovery and is not breaking down in a way that cleanly rewards pressing shorts. Even with the event board down to CAUTION, the cleaner move is still to deal with the weak SOL position first.
ETH remains strong and the only event brake here is a narrow exchange notice, not a broad bearish macro trigger. It is not a cleaner active trade than flattening the deteriorating SOL short.
XPL continues to trend up and is already too extended for a clean fresh long while still not weak enough to short. Best left alone.
LDO is fading slightly from the rebound highs, but not in a way that creates a high-quality short, and positive funding still makes the long side unattractive here. It stays neutral.
MORPHO remains one of the cleaner underlying structures on the sheet, but it still lacks enough independent edge to justify a fresh call while position management is focused on the live SOL risk.
ALGO is still recovering and does not offer a clean fade or an attractive fresh breakout entry after the move it has already made. No action.
IO has rolled off its local highs a bit but remains too mixed to short confidently and too messy to buy. It is not cleaner than simply covering SOL.
TST is still highly extended after its squeeze and remains unstable enough that neither chasing nor fading looks high quality. Watch-only.
KNEIRO still lacks robust live chart context and remains too thin and underdefined for a reliable position-management upgrade.
